prompt:
# ShiftWork Demo Simulation — Cloud Agent Instructions

You are a cloud agent testing Opulent's doc-to-deploy pipeline end to end. You have access to the Opulent platform and its tools. Follow these instructions exactly. At the end, produce a findings report and return it.

## Context

ShiftWork hands a completed training doc to Opulent. Opulent's job is to find the doc, build a visual QA agent from it, run acceptance tests, and deliver the agent on the platform. This simulation tests that full flow.

## Run details

- Run stamp: 20260701-074833
- Marker: SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-074833
- Completion marker: SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE
- Target: staging
- Timeout: 45 minutes
- Target site: https://shiftworkstudio.ai (fallback: https://example.com)

## Fixture

The training doc fixture is at:
/Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json

It contains:
- Business: ShiftWork Studio (Dan Cruden)
- Target URL: https://shiftworkstudio.ai
- Build target: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
- 8 build-critical answers (OP09, VQ01-VQ07) all owner_confirmed
- R01 (email connector) unconfirmed — draft-only email mode
- Gap review completed by shiftwork_crew
- Pipeline state: steps 5-11 completed_by_shiftwork, steps 12-14 not_started_opulent_owns

## Steps to execute

### Step 1: Load Ostack discipline
Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-engineering-discipline. This is mandatory.

### Step 2: Create demo workspace
Create a workspace named "ShiftWork Demo Simulation 20260701-074833". Record the workspaceId.

### Step 3: Create build agent thread
Create a thread in the demo workspace. Record the threadId. This is the build agent thread.

### Step 4: Upload training doc to workspace
Read the fixture at /Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json. Write it to the workspace at /opulent/workspace/uploads/shiftwork-training-doc-SW-20260701-001-20260701-074833.json. Replace the marker value in the JSON with SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-074833. Preserve all other fields.

### Step 5: Send build instruction to agent thread
Send a message to the build agent thread with this prompt:

---
You are a build agent in the Opulent platform. A ShiftWork training doc has been placed in your workspace. Your job is to find it, validate it, build a visual QA agent from it, create a cron schedule, and run acceptance tests.

STEP A: Find the training doc. Search the workspace for a file with schema marker "opulent.shiftwork.training_doc.v1" and leadId "SW-20260701-001". Use list_files and read_file.

STEP B: Validate the doc minimally. Check: (1) buildTarget is present and non-empty, (2) VQ07 answer exists, (3) VQ01 has targetUrl. If any missing, stop and report "cannot build: missing required fields".

STEP C: Run the automation modeler flow. Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-automation-modeler. Follow the intake flow and produce a Verified Automation Report. The automation: "Screenshot https://shiftworkstudio.ai at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844 on a weekday schedule at 8 AM Central, compare against baseline, draft an email alert if a visual issue is found." Use the training doc answers as the spec. If the site is down, use https://example.com as fallback.

STEP D: Build the visual QA agent from the report. The agent: (1) screenshots the target URL at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844, (2) compares against baseline, (3) if issue found, drafts an alert email with subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT containing screenshots, URL, viewport, severity, issue description, fix suggestion, (4) stores screenshots and a run report in the workspace. Draft-only — do not send emails.

STEP E: Create the cron schedule. Call cron_manage action=create with cron="0 8 * * 1-5", timezone="America/Chicago", prompt describing the visual QA run, recurring=true. Record the schedule ID.

STEP F: Run the agent against 5 random pages on the target site. For each: screenshot at desktop and mobile, store in workspace, produce a report entry. All 5 must complete without crashing. If any crash, auto-fix up to 3 tries. After 3 failed fixes, stop and report.

STEP G: Run the acceptance test. Check: (1) at least 4 of 5 pages screenshotted successfully, (2) screenshots stored in workspace, (3) report produced. Must-pass: at least one screenshot + report produced. If 4/5 with must-pass OK, mark PASS. Otherwise FAIL.

STEP H: If acceptance passes, produce a how-to guide as markdown in the workspace. Title: "How to use your Visual QA Agent". Cover: login, see agent, read alerts, trigger manual run, understand schedule. 1-2 pages.

STEP I: Call autos_done with summary including: what was built, schedule ID, acceptance result, artifact locations, and marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-074833.
---

### Step 6: Monitor build agent
Monitor the build agent thread for completion. Check for:
- skill_manage call for opulentia-automation-modeler
- document_create or write_file for Verified Automation Report
- cron_manage action=create
- screenshot artifacts in workspace
- report artifact in workspace
- autos_done call with marker

Use session_manage action=status to check progress. Wait up to 30 minutes.

### Step 7: Collect workspace artifacts
List all files in the workspace. Read the Verified Automation Report, acceptance test report, and how-to guide if they exist. Count screenshots. Record all paths.

### Step 8: Evaluate against pass/fail criteria
Read the pass/fail criteria from:
/Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-pass-fail-criteria.json

For each criterion, mark PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, or SKIP with evidence.

### Step 9: Produce findings report
Generate a findings report using the template at:
/Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-findings-template.json

Fill in all fields. Write the report to:
/Users/jeremyalston/Perfect/opulent-os-heavy/frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-findings-20260701-074833.json

Also write it to the workspace as a document.

### Step 10: Call autos_done with completion marker
Call autos_done with result including SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE and a summary of findings.

## Pass/Fail rules

- PASS: All 14 MUST-PASS criteria pass AND at least 20 of 24 total criteria pass
- PARTIAL: All 14 MUST-PASS criteria pass AND 15-19 of 24 pass
- FAIL: Any MUST-PASS fails OR fewer than 15 pass

## What to return

Return the findings report JSON. Include:
1. Overall verdict (PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL)
2. Per-criterion results with evidence
3. Workspace artifact list
4. Build agent thread URL
5. Errors encountered
6. Recommendations

## Runbook overrides (already decided, do not change)

- Scope decider: auto-decide (no human gate for single vs orchestrated)
- Steps 5-11: ShiftWork's job (not Opulent)
- VAPI: not handled by Opulent (crew call only)
- Customer login: skip for POC
- Runtime payer enforcement: skip for POC
- Dreams in memory: skip for POC
- Email: draft-only (R01 unconfirmed)

## Model strategy

- Build agent: strong model (Opus or Sonnet)
- Running visual QA agent: cheap model (Haiku or open-source)

## Sandbox

- Daytona sandbox with secrets management
- No codebase access, no DOM access
- Screenshots only

scenario.label: ShiftWork doc-to-deploy demo simulation
scenario.description: End-to-end demo simulation of Opulent's doc-to-deploy pipeline. A cloud agent receives a completed training doc from ShiftWork, builds a visual QA agent, runs acceptance tests, and delivers the agent on the Opulent platform. This script contains the full instruction set, pass/fail criteria, and fixture reference for a cloud agent to execute and return findings.

opulentOwns: Receive training doc from shared workspace
Build agent from training doc (automation modeler flow)
Acceptance test (5 random pages, screenshots, report)
Deliver agent on Opulent platform (cron + on-demand)
shiftworkOwns: Onboarding call (crew on Google Meet, not VAPI)

fixture.buildTarget: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
fixture.note: Fixture is a believable payload from ShiftWork after crew call + gap review. URL targets Dan's actual dummy app. Fallback URL is used if shiftworkstudio.ai is down.

instructionSet.summary: You are a cloud agent testing Opulent's doc-to-deploy pipeline end to end. You will: (1) set up a workspace with the training doc, (2) send a build instruction to an Opulent agent thread, (3) monitor the build agent as it finds the doc, validates it, runs the automation modeler flow, builds the visual QA agent, creates a cron schedule, and runs acceptance tests, (4) check results against the pass/fail criteria, (5) return a findings report.

steps.name: Load Ostack discipline
steps.action: Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-engineering-discipline. This is mandatory before any work.
steps.passCriteria: skill_manage result includes 'opulentia-engineering-discipline' with status completed
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed without Ostack discipline.

steps.name: Create demo workspace
steps.action: Create a workspace named 'ShiftWork Demo Simulation 20260701-074833'. Record the workspaceId.
steps.passCriteria: workspace_manage action=create returns a valid workspaceId
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed without a workspace.

steps.name: Create build agent thread
steps.action: Create a thread in the demo workspace. This is the build agent thread that will run the full automation modeler flow. Record the threadId.
steps.passCriteria: workspace_manage action=create_thread returns a valid threadId
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed without a build thread.

steps.name: Upload training doc to workspace
steps.action: Read the fixture file at frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json. Write it to the workspace at /opulent/workspace/uploads/shiftwork-training-doc-SW-20260701-001-20260701-074833.json. Replace the marker value in the JSON with SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-074833. Preserve all other fields exactly.
steps.expectedResult: File written to workspace with marker replaced
steps.passCriteria: write_file succeeds and file is listed in workspace files
steps.onFail: STOP. The training doc must be in the workspace before the build agent can find it.

steps.name: Send build instruction to agent thread
steps.action:
Send a message to the build agent thread with the following prompt. This is the core instruction that drives the entire build flow.

PROMPT START ---
You are a build agent in the Opulent platform. A ShiftWork training doc has been placed in your workspace. Your job is to find it, validate it, build a visual QA agent from it, create a cron schedule, and run acceptance tests. Follow these steps exactly:

STEP A: Find the training doc. Search the workspace for a file with schema marker 'opulent.shiftwork.training_doc.v1' and leadId 'SW-20260701-001'. Use list_files and read_file to find and read it.

STEP B: Validate the doc minimally. Check that: (1) buildTarget is present and non-empty, (2) acceptance test inputs are present (VQ07 answer exists), (3) targetUrl is present in VQ01. If any are missing, stop and report 'cannot build: missing required fields'.

STEP C: Run the full automation modeler flow. Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-automation-modeler. Follow the skill's intake flow: desired automation, connected apps, tools, memory, knowledge, workflow, dry-run evals, and produce a Verified Automation Report. The automation is: 'Screenshot https://shiftworkstudio.ai at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844 on a weekday schedule at 8 AM Central, compare against baseline, draft an email alert if a visual issue is found.' Use the training doc answers as the spec.

STEP D: Build the visual QA agent from the Verified Automation Report. The agent should: (1) screenshot the target URL at desktop and mobile, (2) compare against baseline, (3) if issue found, draft an alert email with subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT containing screenshots, URL, viewport, severity, issue description, and fix suggestion, (4) store screenshots and a run report in the workspace. Draft-only mode — do not send emails. R01 email connector is unconfirmed.

STEP E: Create the cron schedule. Call cron_manage action=create with cron='0 8 * * 1-5', timezone='America/Chicago', prompt describing the visual QA run, recurring=true. Record the schedule ID. Also note that manual on-demand trigger should be available.

STEP F: Run the agent against 5 random pages on shiftworkstudio.ai. For each page: screenshot at desktop and mobile, store screenshots in workspace, produce a report entry. If the site is down, use the fallback URL 'https://example.com'. All 5 must complete without crashing. If any crash, attempt auto-fix (up to 3 tries). After 3 failed fixes, stop and report the error.

STEP G: Run the acceptance test. Check that: (1) at least 4 of 5 pages were screenshotted successfully, (2) screenshots are stored in the workspace, (3) a report was produced. The must-pass case is: at least one screenshot + report produced. If 4/5 pass with must-pass OK, mark acceptance as PASS. Otherwise mark as FAIL.

STEP H: If acceptance passes, produce a how-to guide as a markdown document in the workspace. Title: 'How to use your Visual QA Agent'. Cover: how to log in, see your agent, read alerts, trigger a manual run, understand the schedule. Keep it to 1-2 pages.

STEP I: Call autos_done with a summary of what was built, the schedule ID, the acceptance test result, and the workspace artifact locations. Include the marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_20260701-074833 in the autos_done summary.

PROMPT END ---
steps.expectedResult: Message sent to build agent thread. Agent begins processing.
steps.passCriteria: Message is sent successfully and agent thread starts processing
steps.onFail: STOP. The build agent cannot start without the instruction.

steps.name: Monitor build agent progress
steps.action: Monitor the build agent thread for completion. Check for: (1) skill_manage call for opulentia-automation-modeler, (2) document_create or write_file calls for the Verified Automation Report, (3) cron_manage action=create call, (4) screenshot artifacts in workspace, (5) report artifact in workspace, (6) autos_done call with the completion marker. Use session_manage action=status to check progress. Wait up to 30 minutes for completion.
steps.expectedResult: Agent completes all steps A-I and calls autos_done
steps.onFail: If agent does not complete within 30 minutes, record what steps were completed and what failed. Continue to step 7 to evaluate partial results.

steps.name: Collect workspace artifacts
steps.action: List all files in the workspace. Read the Verified Automation Report if it exists. Read the acceptance test report if it exists. Read the how-to guide if it exists. Count screenshots. Record all artifact paths.
steps.expectedResult: All artifacts produced by the build agent are catalogued
steps.passCriteria: At least: training doc, Verified Automation Report, screenshots (at least 4), run report, how-to guide are present
steps.onFail: Record which artifacts are missing. Continue to step 8 to evaluate against criteria.

steps.name: Evaluate against pass/fail criteria
steps.action: Compare the collected artifacts and agent behavior against the pass/fail criteria in demo-pass-fail-criteria.json. For each criterion, mark PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL with evidence.
steps.passCriteria: validation_check completes with all criteria scored
steps.onFail: Record which criteria could not be evaluated and why.

steps.name: Produce findings report
steps.action: Generate a findings report in JSON format using the demo-findings-template.json structure. Include: scenario ID, run stamp, overall verdict (PASS/FAIL), per-criterion results with evidence, workspace artifact list, agent thread URL, errors encountered, and recommendations.
steps.expectedResult: Findings report written to workspace and local filesystem
steps.passCriteria: Findings report is valid JSON with all fields populated
steps.onFail: Record the failure to produce the report itself as a finding.

steps.name: Call autos_done with completion marker
steps.action: Call autos_done with the completion marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE and a summary of the findings.
steps.onFail: Record failure to call autos_done as a finding.

verificationSignals.description: Build instruction sent to agent thread
Build agent found and read the training doc

verificationSignals.argIncludes: How to use your Visual QA Agent

verificationSignals.description: Build agent called autos_done with marker

runbookOverrides.scopeDecider: Auto-decide from doc (Runbook says human decides — overridden for POC)
runbookOverrides.steps5to11Ownership: ShiftWork (Runbook says Opulent — overridden by user)
runbookOverrides.vapi: Not handled by Opulent (Runbook says Opulent or VAPI — overridden by user)
runbookOverrides.customerLogin: Skip for POC (Runbook requires login — deferred)
runbookOverrides.runtimePayerEnforcement: Skip for POC (Runbook requires enforcement — deferred)
runbookOverrides.dreamsInMemory: Skip for POC (promised in meeting — deferred)

runbookFollows.deliveryOwner: Opulent owns delivery, ShiftWork schedules + pays (followed)
runbookFollows.roi: Opulent exposes logs, ShiftWork does check-in (followed)

modelStrategy.buildAgent: Strong model (Opus or Sonnet) for automation modeler flow
modelStrategy.runningAgent: Cheap model (Haiku or open-source) for visual QA execution
modelStrategy.rationale: Tiered strategy from June 12 meeting. Coordinator on strong model, execution on cheap model.

sandbox.rationale: Promised to Braden in June 12 meeting. Granular access controls, VM sandboxing, secrets management.

emailMode.reason: R01 email connector unconfirmed in training doc. Owner requested draft-only. Live send is post-build wiring.
emailMode.alertContents: Subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT, screenshots, page URL, viewport, severity, issue description, fix suggestion
emailMode.note: Draft-only mode auto-enforces the do-not-email-customers rule because nothing is sent.

targetSite.rationale: Dan's actual dummy app from the June 12 meeting. Fallback if site is down during demo.

acceptanceTest.inputs: 5 random pages on the target site
acceptanceTest.passCriteria: 4/5 pages screenshotted successfully with report produced
acceptanceTest.mustPass: At least one screenshot + report produced
acceptanceTest.fixLoop: Up to 2 rounds, each capped at 2 business days. After 2 fails, human decides: rebuild narrower, partial with credit, or refund.
acceptanceTest.approver: Named human reviews the auto-run score
acceptanceTest.note: 5 easy-to-hit pre-validated test criteria. No seeded defects.

twoAgentsRequired.pocSuccessMeasure: Agents deployed for both Dan Cruden and Braden Silcott
twoAgentsRequired.buildOrder: Sequential. Dan first (shiftworkstudio.ai), Braden second (after intake received).

criteria.description: Pass/fail rubric for the ShiftWork doc-to-deploy demo simulation. Each criterion is scored PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL with evidence. Overall verdict is PASS only if all MUST-PASS criteria pass and at least 80% of all criteria pass.

verdictRules.pass: All MUST-PASS criteria pass AND at least 80% of all criteria pass
verdictRules.partial: All MUST-PASS criteria pass AND 60-79% of all criteria pass
verdictRules.fail: Any MUST-PASS criterion fails OR fewer than 60% of all criteria pass

criteria.name: Ostack discipline loaded
criteria.check: skill_manage was called for opulentia-engineering-discipline and completed successfully
criteria.evidenceRequired: Tool call log showing skill_manage with opulentia-engineering-discipline, status completed
criteria.passCondition: skill_manage result includes 'opulentia-engineering-discipline' with status completed
criteria.failCondition: skill_manage was not called, or failed, or wrong skill was loaded

criteria.name: Demo workspace created
criteria.check: A workspace was created for the demo simulation
criteria.evidenceRequired: workspace_manage action=create returned a valid workspaceId
criteria.passCondition: workspaceId is non-null and non-empty
criteria.failCondition: No workspaceId returned or workspace creation failed

criteria.name: Build agent thread created
criteria.check: A thread was created in the demo workspace for the build agent
criteria.evidenceRequired: workspace_manage action=create_thread returned a valid threadId
criteria.passCondition: threadId is non-null and non-empty
criteria.failCondition: No threadId returned or thread creation failed

criteria.name: Training doc uploaded to workspace
criteria.check: The training doc fixture was written to the workspace with the demo marker
criteria.evidenceRequired: write_file succeeded and file appears in list_files output
criteria.passCondition: File exists in workspace with SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN in content
criteria.failCondition: File was not written or marker is missing

criteria.name: Build instruction sent to agent thread
criteria.check: A message was sent to the build agent thread with the full build instruction prompt
criteria.evidenceRequired: chatDb.sendMessage succeeded with the build instruction prompt
criteria.passCondition: Message sent successfully to the build agent thread
criteria.failCondition: Message was not sent or thread did not receive it

criteria.name: Build agent found the training doc
criteria.check: The build agent searched the workspace and found the training doc by schema marker + leadId
criteria.evidenceRequired: read_file or list_files call in the build agent thread that reads the training doc with schema opulent.shiftwork.training_doc.v1
criteria.passCondition: Build agent read the training doc and the content includes the schema marker and leadId SW-20260701-001
criteria.failCondition: Build agent did not find or read the training doc

criteria.name: Build agent validated the doc minimally
criteria.check: The build agent checked that buildTarget is present, acceptance test inputs (VQ07) are present, and targetUrl is present in VQ01
criteria.evidenceRequired: Build agent transcript shows validation of buildTarget, VQ07, and VQ01 before proceeding to build
criteria.passCondition: All three fields validated as present
criteria.failCondition: Validation was skipped or any required field was not checked
criteria.partialCondition: Some but not all fields were validated

criteria.name: Automation modeler skill invoked
criteria.check: The build agent called skill_manage for opulentia-automation-modeler
criteria.evidenceRequired: skill_manage call in build agent thread with opulentia-automation-modeler
criteria.passCondition: skill_manage was called and completed for opulentia-automation-modeler
criteria.failCondition: Automation modeler was not invoked

criteria.name: Verified Automation Report produced
criteria.check: The build agent produced a Verified Automation Report as a document or file in the workspace
criteria.evidenceRequired: document_create or write_file call that creates a document/file containing 'Verified Automation Report'
criteria.passCondition: Report exists in workspace with title containing 'Verified Automation Report'
criteria.failCondition: No Verified Automation Report was produced

criteria.name: Visual QA agent built from report
criteria.check: The build agent created a visual QA agent configuration or workflow from the Verified Automation Report
criteria.evidenceRequired: createAgentConfig or workflow creation call in the build agent thread
criteria.passCondition: Agent config or workflow was created with visual QA capabilities (screenshot, compare, alert draft)
criteria.failCondition: No agent was built from the report

criteria.name: Cron schedule created
criteria.check: The build agent created a cron schedule for weekday 8 AM Central
criteria.evidenceRequired: cron_manage action=create call with cron='0 8 * * 1-5' and timezone='America/Chicago'
criteria.passCondition: cron_manage returned a schedule ID with the correct cron expression and timezone
criteria.failCondition: No cron schedule was created or the expression/timezone is wrong

criteria.name: On-demand trigger available
criteria.check: The built agent supports manual on-demand trigger from the platform
criteria.evidenceRequired: Agent configuration or workflow includes a manual trigger type, or the agent can be run on-demand
criteria.passCondition: Manual on-demand trigger is available
criteria.failCondition: No on-demand trigger is available
criteria.partialCondition: On-demand is mentioned but not wired

criteria.name: Draft-only email mode
criteria.check: The built agent drafts alert emails but does not send them (R01 unconfirmed)
criteria.evidenceRequired: Agent configuration or run output shows email drafting without sending, or alert draft artifact in workspace
criteria.passCondition: Agent produces email drafts without sending, or no live send is configured
criteria.failCondition: Agent attempts to send live emails

criteria.name: 5 test pages screenshotted
criteria.check: The agent ran against 5 random pages on the target site and captured screenshots
criteria.evidenceRequired: At least 4 screenshot files in the workspace (desktop + mobile for at least 2 pages, or 4 separate page screenshots)
criteria.passCondition: At least 4 of 5 pages screenshotted successfully
criteria.failCondition: Fewer than 4 pages screenshotted

criteria.name: Screenshots stored in workspace
criteria.check: All captured screenshots are stored as files in the workspace
criteria.evidenceRequired: Screenshot files appear in list_files output with image file extensions
criteria.passCondition: Screenshot files exist in workspace
criteria.failCondition: Screenshots were captured but not stored, or no screenshots exist

criteria.name: Run report produced
criteria.check: A run report was produced describing what was found on each page
criteria.evidenceRequired: Report file in workspace with content describing page results, timestamps, URLs, viewports, and verdicts
criteria.passCondition: Report exists with content for at least 4 of 5 pages
criteria.failCondition: No report was produced

criteria.name: Acceptance test evaluated (4/5 rule)
criteria.check: The acceptance test was evaluated with the 4/5 pass rule and must-pass case
criteria.evidenceRequired: validation_check call or agent output that explicitly states PASS or FAIL with the 4/5 count and must-pass status
criteria.passCondition: Acceptance test result is explicitly PASS or FAIL with the count and must-pass evaluation
criteria.failCondition: Acceptance test was not evaluated or result is ambiguous

criteria.name: How-to guide produced
criteria.check: A how-to markdown guide was produced in the workspace
criteria.evidenceRequired: document_create or write_file call that creates a document titled 'How to use your Visual QA Agent'
criteria.passCondition: How-to guide exists in workspace with title containing 'How to use your Visual QA Agent'
criteria.failCondition: No how-to guide was produced

criteria.name: Agent run logs exposed
criteria.check: The agent's run logs (run count, findings, alerts) are accessible from the platform
criteria.evidenceRequired: Agent run history is visible in the thread or workspace, or a run log artifact exists
criteria.passCondition: Run logs are accessible
criteria.failCondition: Run logs are not accessible
criteria.partialCondition: Some run data is visible but not structured as logs

criteria.name: Build agent called autos_done with marker
criteria.check: The build agent called autos_done with the demo marker in its summary
criteria.evidenceRequired: autos_done call in build agent thread with result including SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN
criteria.passCondition: autos_done was called with the marker
criteria.failCondition: autos_done was not called or marker is missing

criteria.name: Findings report produced
criteria.check: A findings report was produced with overall verdict, per-criterion results, and evidence
criteria.evidenceRequired: Findings report JSON file with all fields populated
criteria.passCondition: Findings report is valid JSON with overall verdict, all criteria scored, and evidence for each
criteria.failCondition: No findings report was produced or it is incomplete

criteria.name: Completion marker emitted
criteria.check: The demo simulation completed with the SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE marker
criteria.evidenceRequired: autos_done call with result including SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE
criteria.passCondition: Completion marker was emitted
criteria.failCondition: Completion marker was not emitted

criteria.name: Fallback URL used if primary is down
criteria.check: If shiftworkstudio.ai was unreachable, the agent used the fallback URL https://example.com
criteria.evidenceRequired: Agent transcript or report shows fallback URL usage if primary was down, or primary was reachable so fallback was not needed
criteria.passCondition: Either primary URL worked, or fallback was used when primary was down
criteria.failCondition: Primary was down and no fallback was used
criteria.skipCondition: Primary URL was reachable (fallback not needed — mark as PASS with note)

criteria.name: No React #185 crash
criteria.check: No React error #185 (Maximum update depth exceeded) occurred during the demo
criteria.evidenceRequired: No 'Minified React error #185' or 'Maximum update depth exceeded' in any page text, screenshot, or browser console output
criteria.passCondition: No React #185 crash occurred
criteria.failCondition: React #185 crash occurred at any point during the demo

summary.passThreshold: All 14 MUST-PASS pass AND at least 80% of all 24 pass (>= 20)
summary.failThreshold: Any MUST-PASS fails OR fewer than 15 criteria pass

callInfo.callRoutingNote: Crew call on Google Meet. VAPI is not handled by Opulent. ShiftWork owns the call and recording.

buildTarget.target: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
buildTarget.ownerStatement: I want an agent to screenshot shiftworkstudio.ai on desktop and mobile and email me when something looks broken.
buildTarget.provisionalTargetFromResearch: Ops admin automation for invoice and inbox triage
buildTarget.changeReason: Owner's top felt problem is catching landing page breakage before customers notice, not invoice chasing. Research brief flagged this as likely core problem. Owner confirmed it out loud.

answers.question: How do invoices go out today, and how do you chase a late payment?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely QuickBooks or paper invoices based on industry norms. Chase process unknown.
answers.ownerAnswer: I send invoices through QuickBooks after the deposit hits. If someone is late I send an email at 15 days, a text at 30 days, and I call at 45 days. Honestly I hate chasing people.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: How much money sits unpaid past due in a normal month?
answers.draftAnswer: Unknown. Research could not find public data on accounts receivable.
answers.ownerAnswer: Probably 8 to 12 thousand in a normal month. It is usually one or two jobs where the final invoice is sitting unpaid.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What lands in your inbox that you wish you didn't have to sort?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely spam, vendor emails, and lead form confirmations based on industry patterns.
answers.ownerAnswer: Vendor emails, spam, Google review notifications, and lead form confirmations from the website. The lead confirmations matter but I do not need to read them one by one.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: Which emails actually need you, and which could be filed or handled for you?
answers.ownerAnswer: Customer complaints, estimate requests, and anything from my crew leads needs me. Everything else could be filed or summarized. Vendor emails can be auto-filed.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: How do you schedule your team and your work today?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely a mix of text messages and a shared calendar based on industry norms.
answers.ownerAnswer: I use a Google Calendar for my own schedule and a group text for the crew. It breaks down when two jobs overlap or someone calls out sick.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What goes wrong with scheduling in a normal week?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely double-books, last-minute changes, and no-shows based on industry patterns.
answers.ownerAnswer: Double-books when I forget to update the calendar after a text conversation. Last-minute reschedules from customers. Crew no-shows maybe once a month.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: How do you hire today, and where does it bog down?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely Indeed and referrals based on industry norms.
answers.ownerAnswer: Word of mouth and referrals mostly. Sometimes Indeed. It bogs down at screening because I do not have time to call everyone back.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What makes someone a good fit, and what's a quick no?
answers.ownerAnswer: Good fit is someone who shows up on time, has done residential work before, and does not complain about the drive. Quick no is anyone who no-shows the first interview or lies about experience.
answers.gapReviewNote: Not build-critical for the visual QA agent. Answered on call. No gap.

answers.question: What admin task eats the most of your time each week?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely checking whether the landing page still looks right after edits, based on the build request.
answers.ownerAnswer: Checking whether the landing page still looks right after edits. I usually notice problems only after a customer says the quote button is missing or a photo is broken.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed on call. This is the top felt problem that drives the first build.

answers.question: What page should the visual quality agent monitor first?
answers.draftAnswer: Monitor https://shiftworkstudio.ai first based on the business domain.
answers.ownerAnswer: Monitor https://shiftworkstudio.ai first. The highest priority route is the home landing page at / because that is where paid traffic lands.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed URL and priority route on call.

answers.question: Which viewports matter?
answers.draftAnswer: Desktop and mobile based on industry norms. Exact dimensions unknown.
answers.ownerAnswer: Check desktop at 1280 by 900 and mobile at 390 by 844. Mobile matters most because most leads come from phones.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed exact viewport dimensions on call.

answers.question: What counts as a visual inconsistency?
answers.ownerAnswer: Email me if text overlaps, the hero image is missing, the Get a Quote button is hidden or unreachable, there is horizontal scroll on mobile, a section is blank, navigation covers content, the form is cut off, or the page has console errors.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner provided the full inconsistency criteria list on call.

answers.question: What should the email alert include?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely a screenshot and a description of the problem.
answers.ownerAnswer: Send screenshots, the page URL, viewport size, what changed, how severe it is, and a short plain-English fix suggestion. Put VISUAL QA ALERT in the subject.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed alert contents and subject prefix on call.

answers.question: Who should receive alerts?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely the owner email based on the lead record.
answers.ownerAnswer: Send alerts to dan@shiftworkstudio.ai. Do not email customers.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed recipient and the do-not-email-customers rule on call.

answers.question: How often should the agent run?
answers.ownerAnswer: Run every weekday at 8:00 AM Central and also on demand when I ask for a check. For the first build, manual on-demand is acceptable if scheduling is not wired yet.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed schedule and manual fallback on call.

answers.question: What should the first successful run prove?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely that screenshots are captured and an alert is produced when an issue is found.
answers.ownerAnswer: It should capture desktop and mobile screenshots of the landing page, compare them to the baseline, find no critical issue or describe the issue, and produce the exact alert email draft if anything is wrong.
answers.gapReviewNote: Build-critical. Owner confirmed acceptance test on call.

answers.question: Which email connector should the agent use?
answers.draftAnswer: Likely Outlook or Gmail based on the owner email.
answers.ownerAnswer: I use Gmail for my business email. But I am not sure about giving an agent access to it. Can it just draft the email and I send it?
answers.gapReviewNote: RESEARCH GAP. Owner mentioned Gmail but did not confirm connector access. Owner requested draft-only mode. This stays unconfirmed. Live email send requires explicit approval and connector setup.

gapReview.signOffNote: All 8 build-critical questions are owner-confirmed. R01 (email connector) remains unconfirmed. Owner requested draft-only email mode. Build can proceed with draft-only email. Live send is a post-build wiring step.

storageLocation.note: Training doc lives in the shared Opulent workspace. Build agent finds it by schema marker + leadId. Recording is ShiftWork-owned, not read by the build agent.

intakeAnswerReference.relationship: The intake answer is the pre-call artifact. This training doc is the post-call artifact. The intake answer carries draft answers tagged draft_from_research_unconfirmed. This training doc carries the same answers updated to owner_confirmed or corrected_by_owner, plus the confirmed build target, plus the gap review sign-off.

react185Status.evidence: No React error #185 found in any output
